title: spectracle – OMIP-109
text: Derive spectra using OMIP-109 source data.

title: spectracle – OMIP-109
text: Source data presented OMIP-109 downloaded public repository: figshare – OMIP-109
code: ## manually download or follow this script  ## source directory; this path is user-dependent; ## the author of this article previously downloaded these source files to: dir.source <- "W:/OMIP-109_flowstate/data_source/OMIP-XXX 45-Color Experiment 1/" ## these source files were processed using spectracle(); ## the results were saved to an .RDS solely for building this article  ## BELOW IS THE METHOD USED TO DOWNLOAD/VERIFY SOURCE  ## figshare name: 45-Color Full Spectrum Flow Cytometry Panel ## Experiment ($PROJ) name: OMIP-XXX 45-Color Experiment 1 ## Size: 1.74 GB ## MD5 checksum: 623c49ca26c4584a3bcc44b6a3fa5843 ## download link: https://ndownloader.figshare.com/files/45873810 ## Citation: Jaimes, Maria (2024). 45-Color Full Spectrum Flow Cytometry Panel.  ## figshare. Dataset. https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.25699221.v1   ## test for existence and integrity of source files (.fcs) if(dir.exists(dir.source)){   digests <- sapply(list.files(dir.source, full.names = T, recursive = T, pattern = ".fcs"),                     digest::digest,                     file = TRUE)   digest <- digest::digest(as.vector(digests))   if(digest != "fbe1796cfccead8c4096f942826f6613"){     message("Digest of source files (.fcs) does not match historic value;\ndownloading source files...")     download.source <- TRUE   }else{     download.source <- FALSE   } }else{   download.source <- TRUE } if(download.source){   temp <- tempfile(tmpdir = "data_temp/", fileext = ".zip")   zip.link <- "https://ndownloader.figshare.com/files/45873810"   curl::curl_download(     url = zip.link,     destfile = temp,     quiet = FALSE,     mode = "wb"   )   ## check the hash   if (tools::md5sum(temp) != "623c49ca26c4584a3bcc44b6a3fa5843") {     stop("MD5 checksum (hash) of the downloaded .zip does not match the historic value.")   }   ## files contained in the .zip: 100 files;   ## Raw, Unmixed, and SpectroFlo software files (.Expt, .WTML, and .UST)   utils::unzip(temp, list = TRUE)   ## unzip to "./data_temp"   utils::unzip(temp, files = NULL, exdir = "./data_temp/")   ## get the original 'Project' ('$PROJ') name to reconstruct directory structure   proj <- "./data_temp/Raw/Reference Group/Unstained (Cells).fcs" |>     flowstate:::readFCStext() |>     flowstate:::keywords.to.data.table() |>     _[][['$PROJ']]   ## R scripts directory   dir.analysis <- file.path("R", proj)   if (!dir.exists(dir.analysis))     dir.create(dir.analysis)   ## create the source directory   dir.out <- file.path("data_source/", proj)   if (!dir.exists(dir.out))     dir.create(dir.out)   ## move the source .fcs files; Raw and Unmixed   fcs.to.move <- list.files(     "data_temp/",     full.names = T,     pattern = ".fcs",     recursive = T   )   dirnames <- dirname(fcs.to.move) |> unique()   sapply(dirnames, function(i) {     dir.out.sub <- sub("data_temp", dir.out, i)     if (!dir.exists(dir.out.sub))       dir.create(dir.out.sub, recursive = T)   }) |> invisible()   file.rename(from = fcs.to.move,               to = sub("data_temp", dir.out, fcs.to.move)) |> invisible()   ## move the SpectroFlo experiment files   dir.out.expt <- file.path(dir.out, "SpectroFlo_experiment")   if (!dir.exists(dir.out.expt))     dir.create(dir.out.expt)   files.to.move <- list.files("data_temp/", full.names = T, pattern = "Expt|UST|WTML")   file.rename(from = files.to.move,               to = sub("data_temp", dir.out.expt, files.to.move)) |> invisible()   ## cleanup   unlink(temp)   unlink("data_temp/Raw/", recursive = T)   unlink("data_temp/Unmixed//", recursive = T) } ## res <- readRDS(   system.file(     "extdata/prepared_spectra/spectracle_OMIP-109.rds",     package = "spectracle"   ) ) # dir.source.raw <- grep("Raw.*Ref", list.dirs(dir.source), value = T) # raw.reference.controls <- list.files( #   dir.source.raw,  #   full.names = T,  #   pattern = ".fcs" # ) ## data.frame(   "./Raw/Reference Group/" = res$raw.reference.controls,    check.names = F )

title: spectracle – OMIP-109

title: spectracle – OMIP-109
text: spectracle() fully automated design limited function arguments; simply pass directory source files contained. spectracle() returns standardized data.table::data.table() containing relevant metadata: deriving function date--acquisition cytometer, serial number, software sample identifier (sample name, fluor, marker/stain, peak detector, laser) derived, normalized [0,1] spectra hash summaries spectra Derived using fully data-driven/automated process, resultant spectra reproduce given source data function version. summary digest generated individual hashes: Hash (md5) summary (historic): cc8be5bd56a9f25337ce09d68ff80a45 UV V B YG R AF CD11b Spark UV387 (Cells) CD45RA BUV395 (Cells) LIVE DEAD Blue (Cells) CD16 BUV496 (Cells) CCR5 BUV563 (Cells) CD314 BUV615 (Cells) CD39 BUV661 (Cells) CD38 BUV737 (Cells) CD8 BUV805 +BSB (Cells)          CCR7 BV421 (Cells) CD123 Super Bright 436 (Cells) CD11c eFluor 450 (Cells) HLA-DR BV480 (Cells) CD3 BV510 (Cells) CD20 Spark Violet 538 (Cells) IgM BV570 (Cells) IgG BV605 (Cells) CD28 BV650 (Cells) CCR6 BV711 (Cells) CXCR5 BV750 (Cells) PD-1 BV785 (Cells)             TIM-3 BB515 (Cells) CD57 cFluor B532 (Cells) CD14 Spark Blue 550 (Cells) CD2 PerCP-Cy5.5 (Cells) TCRgd PerCP-Vio700 (Cells) CD4 PerCP-Fire 806 (Cells) DNAM-1 RealBlue 780 (Cells)        CD56 cFluor YG584 (Cells) GRP56 PE (Cells) CD24 cFluor YG610 (Cells) CD337 PE-Dazzle594 (Cells) CD103 PE-Fire 640 250ng (Cells) CD45 PerCP (Cells) CD95 PE-Cy5 (Cells) CD25 cFluor BYG710 (Cells) IgD cFluor BYG750 (Cells) CXCR3 PE-Cy7 (Cells) TIGIT PE-Fire 810 (Cells)            CD161 APC 500ng (Cells) CD1c Alexa Fluor 647 (Cells) CD19 Spark NIR685 (Cells) CD127 cFluor R720 (Cells) CD27 APC-H7 (Cells) KLRG-1 APC-Fire 810 (Cells)       Unstained (Cells)
code: # spectra <- spectracle(dir.source.raw) spectra <- res$spectra spectra[] ## hash (md5) summary for this instance spectra[, digest::digest(hash.md5)] #> [1] "cc8be5bd56a9f25337ce09d68ff80a45" spectra[, .(N, S, detector, hash.md5)]

what: Making a Spectacle of Your Spectra
title: Derive Spectra from Full Spectrum Cytometry Controls
text: series figures visual representation can accomplished using spectracle – FULLY AUTOMATED method processing single-color full spectrum cytometry controls. source data used generate figures single-stained reference control (mouse spleen) – BUV615::Siglec F; primary data (shown), rare, truly positive events (number less ~100) obscured /directly impacted primary (V7) secondary (UV7) autofluorescence (AF).
code: ## simply locate a directory containing raw reference controls raw.reference.controls <- list.dirs(path = ".") ## execute the function spectra <- spectracle(raw.reference.controls)

title: Plot a Spectral Trace — plot_trace

title: Plot a Spectral Trace — plot_trace
text: spectra data.table – return spectracle. benchmark logical – default FALSE; TRUE, derived spectra benchmarked internal reference library closest match (cosine similarity) reported black, dashed trace caption text.

title: Plot a Spectral Trace — plot_trace
text: plot printed active device.

title: Plot a Spectral Trace — plot_trace
code: ## due to large file sizes, the .fcs files used in this example were first ## fully processed -- spectra was derived and saved; load here  spectra.files <- list.files( system.file("extdata/prepared_spectra", package = "spectracle"), full.names = TRUE ) names(spectra.files) <- sub(".rds", "", basename(spectra.files))  spectra.examples <- lapply(spectra.files, readRDS)  spectra <- spectra <- spectra.examples$spectra_expt2  plot_trace(spectra[sample.id == "CD16 BUV496 (Cells)"]) plot_trace(spectra[sample.id == "CD16 BUV496 (Cells)"], benchmark = TRUE)

what: Derive Spectra from Full Spectrum Cytometry Controls — spectracle
title: Derive Spectra from Full Spectrum Cytometry Controls — spectracle
text: Manufacturer::Cytometer::Software tested: Cytek::Aurora(5L)::SpectroFlo 3.3.0 fully data-driven/automated process – best performance – spectracle relies adherence established naming conventions appropriate identification unstained control, parsing keyword-value pairs factored metadata, accurate annotation derived spectra.

title: Derive Spectra from Full Spectrum Cytometry Controls — spectracle
code: spectracle(   raw.reference.controls,   filter.top.expressing = NULL,   top.expressing.override = NULL,   print.timings = FALSE,   ... )

title: Derive Spectra from Full Spectrum Cytometry Controls — spectracle
text: raw.reference.controls character vector full directory/path names raw single-color control .fcs files; one list.dirs list.files. filter.top.expressing character vector – default NULL; modulate automated selection 'spectral.events', supplied character vector match unique .fcs sample name(s). defined, conditional filtering take place return 0.99 (cosine) similar 'spectral.events'. top.expressing.override named numeric vector – default NULL; override automated selection 'spectral.events', name(s) supplied numeric vector match unique .fcs sample name(s). Example follows: c("BUV615 (Cells)" = 20); 'BUV615', 20 top expressing events used characterize/derive final spectra (see example ). print.timings logical – default FALSE; TRUE, tictoc print console, detailing execution times. ... defined; placeholder.

title: Derive Spectra from Full Spectrum Cytometry Controls — spectracle
text: data.table normalized [0,1] spectra; additional generalized autofluoresence signature appended table.

title: Derive Spectra from Full Spectrum Cytometry Controls — spectracle
code: ## due to large file sizes, the .fcs files used in this example were first ## fully processed -- spectra was derived and saved; load here  spectra.files <- list.files( system.file("extdata/prepared_spectra", package = "spectracle"), full.names = TRUE ) names(spectra.files) <- sub(".rds", "", basename(spectra.files))  spectra.examples <- lapply(spectra.files, readRDS)  ## expt1  spectra <- spectra.examples$spectra_expt1_nofilter plot_trace(spectra[N == "eFluor 450"])   ## this particular control is low-event count/rare ## impacted by AF -- the spectra is off... plot_trace(spectra[N == "BUV615"])   ## ...rerun with a small tweak using: ## `filter.top.expressing = c("BUV615 (Cells)")` spectra <- spectra.examples$spectra_expt1_filtered  ## spectra is derived plot_trace(spectra[N == "BUV615"])    ## expt2 -- beads and cells spectra <- spectra <- spectra.examples$spectra_expt2  plot_trace(spectra)      spectra[, .(N, hash.md5)] #>         N                         hash.md5 #>    <fctr>                           <char> #> 1: BUV496 8888993b42bdb2339d32e80ef1ac8352 #> 2: BUV496 fc04c76e893f834bd73223397784dfb1 #> 3:     AF 0c1b2ff04128f32c2f9841189acd7b65 #> 4:     AF 5fc0af6b7eb92b44b1b7c8bb29246fd4
